capture_method: Manual local replay against the committed routing fixture and the extracted public-draft skill contract.

observed_output_sections.heading: current navigation problem
observed_output_sections.content: The prompt is about top-level ownership drift: pending work, integrations, and settings are scattered across several tabs, so the miniapp no longer answers where users should go first.

observed_output_sections.heading: proposed hub structure
observed_output_sections.content: The replay answer routes toward one hub tab with explicit internal sections for pending work and settings, keeping the hub summary-driven instead of collapsing everything into one giant page.

observed_output_sections.heading: page ownership and migration map
observed_output_sections.content: The answer maps old top-level destinations into new hub-owned summaries versus surviving detail pages, which keeps the change architectural rather than purely visual.

observed_output_sections.heading: migration order
observed_output_sections.content: The answer sequences the work by freezing top-level tabs, defining hub sections, remapping old entries, and only then removing obsolete navigation, which matches the public workflow.

notes: Captured as transcript-backed replay evidence for the new public-draft architecture skill before cross-repo forward-testing is recorded.
